title: Fictional AI Ops System Review Graph
one_line: Code-review graph shows the files; this system-review graph shows how evidence becomes recommended action, outcome, and lesson.
scope: A fictional public-safe AI operations system used as an open-source example.
bigger_picture: This example shows how a team can explain a complex system without exposing private databases. The system collects safe source summaries, stores evidence contracts, recommends actions, blocks risky operations behind human review, captures outcomes, and turns lessons into better rules.

schemas.name: SourceEnvelope
schemas.purpose: Describes a source observation without exposing raw vendor payloads.
schemas.privacy_notes: Raw source bodies and credentials are never included.

schemas.name: EvidenceFact
schemas.purpose: Stores traceable evidence used by recommendations.
schemas.privacy_notes: Payload should be redacted or fake in public examples.

schemas.name: RecommendationCard
schemas.purpose: Explains why the system recommends an action.

schemas.name: ActionIntent
schemas.purpose: Represents a proposed action before execution.

schemas.name: OutcomeRecord
schemas.purpose: Captures whether action created the intended result.

schemas.name: LessonRecord
schemas.purpose: Turns outcomes into validated or parked rule changes.

artifacts.name: Action Queue
artifacts.purpose: Downstream actions waiting for execution or review.

artifacts.name: Outcome Ledger
artifacts.purpose: Reality feedback from executed or skipped actions.

artifacts.name: Lesson Registry
artifacts.purpose: Validated lessons and parked rule changes.

artifacts.name: Operator Console
artifacts.purpose: Shows recommendations, blockers, approvals, outcomes, and lessons.

decision_gates.name: Source Rights Gate
decision_gates.risk_boundary: No downstream product or public claim uses a source unless rights are clear.

decision_gates.name: Recommendation Confidence Gate
decision_gates.risk_boundary: Weak or ungrounded recommendations cannot become action intents.

decision_gates.name: Human Action Gate
decision_gates.risk_boundary: External sends, money movement, deletion, legal claims, and user-visible changes need approval.

decision_gates.name: Lesson Promotion Gate
decision_gates.risk_boundary: One good outcome is not enough to change future system behavior.

rules.if: repeated positive outcomes and validation pass

systems.name: Source Mesh
systems.purpose: Collects sanitized source summaries from internal and public systems.
systems.truth_boundary: Source summaries are not product claims.
systems.ideal_target: Every source has freshness, rights, and retry metadata.

systems.name: Evidence Core
systems.purpose: Stores facts, relationships, and reasoning traces used by recommendations.
systems.truth_boundary: Database remains private; public report exposes only schema and examples.
systems.ideal_target: Every recommendation can trace back to facts and sources.

systems.name: Recommendation Engine
systems.purpose: Turns evidence into reviewable recommendation cards.
systems.truth_boundary: A recommendation is not an executed action.
systems.ideal_target: Every recommendation has evidence, confidence, failure modes, and next action.

systems.name: Action Engine
systems.purpose: Routes approved recommendations into bounded action intents.
systems.truth_boundary: Restricted actions require approval before execution.
systems.ideal_target: Every action intent has an outcome slot.

systems.name: Outcome Learning Loop
systems.purpose: Captures what happened and converts outcomes into lessons.
systems.truth_boundary: A lesson does not change future behavior until promotion is validated.
systems.ideal_target: Knowledge becomes wisdom through observed outcomes.

systems.name: Operator UI
systems.purpose: Shows recommendations, approvals, outcomes, and lessons without owning truth.
systems.lifecycle: reports -> UI -> operator decision -> action intent
systems.truth_boundary: UI reads canonical reports and requests actions; it does not invent truth.
systems.ideal_target: Operator can run the day from one evidence-backed control room.

workflows.name: Write Evidence
workflows.purpose: Persist traceable facts without exposing private records.

workflows.name: Capture Outcome
workflows.purpose: Record what happened after action or no-action.

edges.why: Operator UI handles restricted action review.

architecture_patterns.name: Open-source library
architecture_patterns.works_for: Python, JavaScript, Rust, Go, Java, or mixed-language libraries
architecture_patterns.mapping: Map public APIs as artifacts, type contracts as schemas, CI/release checks as gates, and examples as walkthroughs.
architecture_patterns.redaction: Usually safe to share full paths and examples.

architecture_patterns.name: Private enterprise microservice
architecture_patterns.works_for: Teams that cannot expose databases or production internals
architecture_patterns.mapping: Map services, APIs, queues, and sanitized contracts. Use logical names for private infrastructure.
architecture_patterns.redaction: Publish schema-only or counts-only artifacts for sensitive stores.

architecture_patterns.name: Data platform
architecture_patterns.works_for: Warehouses, data lakes, ELT/ETL pipelines, reporting systems
architecture_patterns.mapping: Map source loaders, quality checks, marts, dashboards, rights/freshness gates, and report outputs.
architecture_patterns.redaction: Expose table contracts and quality counts, not raw records.

architecture_patterns.name: AI or agent system
architecture_patterns.works_for: LLM apps, agent swarms, RAG systems, ML workflows
architecture_patterns.mapping: Map tools, memory, prompts, retrieval artifacts, eval gates, human approvals, and outcome loops.
architecture_patterns.redaction: Remove secrets, user content, private prompts, and production traces.

architecture_patterns.name: Embedded or microarchitecture-style system
architecture_patterns.works_for: Firmware, hardware interfaces, compiler/runtime internals, chips, robotics
architecture_patterns.mapping: Map interface contracts, telemetry, safety thresholds, state machines, and fault gates.
architecture_patterns.redaction: Publish logical interfaces and safety boundaries, not proprietary layouts or confidential specs.

walkthroughs.name: From source to action
walkthroughs.story: A source rollup says billing-delay tickets increased. The evidence graph stores a redacted fact. The recommendation engine creates a card. The action engine proposes customer communication. The human action gate requires approval because this is external communication.

walkthroughs.name: From outcome to lesson
walkthroughs.story: An approved action produces a positive outcome. The lesson loop records that early customer communication reduced repeat tickets. The lesson is parked until repeated validation proves the rule should be promoted.

example.lesson: early communication may reduce repeat tickets

walkthroughs.name: Private database review
walkthroughs.story: The database is never exposed. Reviewers see the EvidenceFact contract, redaction policy, source counts, and decision gates. They can understand behavior without seeing private rows.

review_questions: Which source and evidence artifacts prove each recommendation?
Which decision gate blocks unsafe downstream action?
Which actions require human approval and why?
Where is the outcome captured after action or no-action?
Which lessons are promoted, parked, or killed based on reality feedback?
What can be safely reviewed if the production database remains private?

rebuild_recipe.goal: Read the system as an operating map.

known_boundaries: This report explains architecture and system behavior; it does not prove production correctness.
Sanitized examples must not be treated as real data.
A passing gate in a report still needs implementation tests in the actual system.
Human/legal/security gates should be implemented in production code, not only documented.
